title: Bonus Episode: Errol Morris on ‘Dr. Strangelove,’ Donald Trump, LSD, and America
format: Sean Fennessey talks with documentarian Errol Morris about his new Showtime film on Joanna Harcourt-Smith and Timothy Leary, and about memory, denial, Trump, and the movies across his career.

films.title: My Psychedelic Love Story
films.note: The new Showtime film, finished the day before this taping. A first-person portrait of Joanna Harcourt-Smith, Timothy Leary's common-law wife, who died six weeks before release. Morris calls it his first love story.

films.title: The Fog of War
films.note: The McNamara film Morris frames as the kin to the new one: a single-subject investigation of self by self.

films.title: The Unknown Known
films.note: The Rumsfeld companion piece to Fog of War (Fennessey misdates it 2014).

films.title: The Thin Blue Line
films.note: Morris's recommended starting point; the mystery he says he was lucky enough to actually solve.

films.title: Mr. Death: The Rise and Fall of Fred A. Leuchter, Jr.
films.note: Morris calls it a failure of nerve: a Harvard rough-cut left students doubting the Holocaust, so he added experts to tell viewers what to think, against his one-subject instinct.

films.title: American Dharma
films.note: The Steve Bannon film savaged after Venice as cancel-culture fodder. Morris says his aim was to show these people offer only destruction, and calls Trump the 'fuck you president' to Bannon's face in it.

films.title: Dr. Strangelove
films.note: The George C. Scott / Peter Sellers 'this person is psychotic' beat anchors Morris's Globe editorial on Trump: obvious, but not obvious enough that people will say it.

films.title: Rashomon
films.note: Morris rejects the received reading of it as an essay on subjective truth; he sees a film about the denial and hiding of truth.

films.title: The B-Side: Elsa Dorfman's Portrait Photography
films.note: His film about photographer Elsa Dorfman, who watched it through her last week of life.

films.title: City Hall
films.note: The new Frederick Wiseman documentary Morris was asked to write about; occasion for their running 'king of misanthropic cinema' argument.

films.title: Vernon, Florida
films.note: Cited as the flattering comparison for the FX series: a true-crime version of it, with a wild array of crazy characters.

films.title: Searching for Bobby Fischer
films.note: The film Morris says he hated for selling a false choice between genius and a normal life; his foil for why he loves 'The Queen's Gambit.'

Mr. Death: The Rise and Fall of Fred A..title: Mr. Death: The Rise and Fall of Fred A. Leuchter, Jr.

Dr. Strangelove.title: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
